name: Camino Francés: El Burgo Ranero and Calzada del Coto Loop
name_original: Calzada del Coto desde El Burgo — circular por el Camino Francés
description: Hike the Calzada del Coto from El Burgo, a difficult 17.9-mile circular route on the Camino Francés, offering solitude and historical sites.
region_intro: Hiking around El Burgo Ranero offers a distinct experience across the flat Castilian plateau, characterized by vast agricultural lands and the open Meseta landscape. The region's terrain primarily consists of long, relatively level stretches, providing a unique setting for long-distance walking. These routes often follow ancient paths, including sections of the historic Camino Francés and Camino Real. The landscape is defined by its expansive plains, offering a sense of solitude and connection with the rural environment. Best hiking trails around El Burgo Ranero The most popular hiking route is Camino Francés: El Burgo Ranero and Calzada del Coto Loop, a 17.9 miles (28.7 km) trail that takes 7 hours 21 minutes to complete, offering a challenging long-distance experience. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Burgo Ranero — circular by Camino Real (Francés), a moderate 8.9 miles (14.3 km) path. This route follows historical paths and is completed in about 3 hours 37 minutes. Local hikers also love the El Burgo Ranero – Earth Houses of Reliegos loop from Fuente del Piojo, a 17.4 miles (28.0 km) trail leading through the open plains towards the unique earth houses of Reliegos, often completed in about 7 hours 10 minutes. Hiking around El Burgo Ranero is defined by its expansive plains, agricultural fields, and historical pilgrimage routes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate day hikes to more challenging long-distance trails. The routes in El Burgo Ranero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 2.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 100 hikers have used komoot to explore El Burgo Ranero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in El Burgo Ranero?
A: There are 9 hiking trails available around El Burgo Ranero, offering a range of experiences across the flat Castilian plateau.

Q: What is the typical terrain like for hiking around El Burgo Ranero?
A: The terrain around El Burgo Ranero is characterized by the vast, flat Castilian Meseta and extensive agricultural lands. Hikers can expect long, relatively level stretches, often following ancient paths like the Camino Francés and Camino Real, offering a unique sense of solitude and connection with the rural environment.

Q: Are there hiking options for different skill levels in El Burgo Ranero?
A: Yes, El Burgo Ranero offers routes for various skill levels. There is 1 easy route, 5 moderate routes, and 3 difficult routes, ensuring options for both casual walkers and those seeking a more challenging long-distance experience.

Q: What historical or natural landmarks can I see while hiking near El Burgo Ranero?
A: While hiking, you can encounter several points of interest. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4547281" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parish Church of San Pedro&lt;/a&gt;, located directly on the Camino, is known for its 16th-century altarpiece and stork nests in late May. The striking ocher-painted Chapel of Virgen de Gracia is another frequently photographed monument. You might also cross the Esla river via an eight-arched bridge. Further afield, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4441511" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Benito Arch and Ruins of the Monastery of San Benito&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5838156" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of Perales&lt;/a&gt; are notable historical sites.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available in El Burgo Ranero?
A: Yes, there are circular routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1177015326/el-burgo-ranero-circular-by-camino-real-frances" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo Ranero — circular by Camino Real (Francés)&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 8.9-mile (14.3 km) path that follows historical routes and takes about 3 hours 37 minutes to complete.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in El Burgo Ranero?
A: Given the open Meseta landscape, spring and autumn generally offer the most comfortable hiking conditions with milder temperatures. Summer can be very hot and exposed, while winter can bring cold temperatures, though the flat terrain makes it accessible year-round for those prepared for the weather.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options from El Burgo Ranero?
A: Yes, El Burgo Ranero is a significant stop on the Camino Francés, a renowned long-distance pilgrimage route. You can undertake stages like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1176915687/camino-frances-el-burgo-ranero-and-calzada-del-coto-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: El Burgo Ranero and Calzada del Coto Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a challenging 17.9-mile (28.7 km) trail that takes over 7 hours to complete.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in El Burgo Ranero?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 2.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often appreciate the unique experience of traversing the vast, open plains of the Meseta, the historical significance of the Camino routes, and the sense of solitude these trails provide.

Q: Are there any unique cultural sights to explore in El Burgo Ranero?
A: Beyond the churches, El Burgo Ranero is notable for its over 70 traditional 'bodegas'—storage rooms dug into the clay soil—offering a glimpse into local agricultural heritage. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38160516" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Burgo Ranero – Earth Houses of Reliegos loop from Fuente del Piojo&lt;/a&gt; leads towards the unique earth houses of Reliegos, providing a distinct cultural experience.

Q: Are the hiking trails around El Burgo Ranero suitable for families or beginners?
A: Yes, there is 1 easy route available, making it suitable for beginners or families looking for a less strenuous walk. Many of the trails are relatively flat, which can be manageable for those new to hiking, though some routes are longer and rated moderate to difficult.

Q: What amenities are available for hikers in El Burgo Ranero?
A: As a key stop on the Camino de Santiago, El Burgo Ranero provides essential services for hikers. These include various accommodations for pilgrims, shops for supplies, and other basic amenities to support your journey.

Q: Can I find trails that offer views of local wildlife or natural features?
A: While the landscape is primarily agricultural, the open plains offer opportunities for birdwatching. The Parish Church of San Pedro's tower is particularly known for its stork nests, especially in late May. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010454" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Station loop from Tierras de la autovía 8&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 9.8-mile (15.8 km) route that might offer specific opportunities for observing local fauna.
tips: In just one kilometer towards Calzada we find a rest area with a Pilgrim's Fountain and a picnic area.

Calzada del Coto is a small town on the alternative French Way and one of the first points after leaving Sahagùn towards Santiago. A great place to have a coffee and rest next to the Church of San Esteban. There is also the San Roque pilgrims' hostel if you are going to spend the night.
Beautifully located Ermita. Unfortunately it was closed.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This circular route is rated as difficult and requires "very good fitness". While the broader Camino Francés in this region is often considered flatter, this specific deviation likely includes more strenuous sections or rougher paths, making it more challenging than the main pilgrim route.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses the "Páramo leonés" or Leonese plateau, characterized by vast plains and extensive crop fields, often called the "breadbasket of Spain". While generally flat or gently undulating, some sections, especially on alternative routes, can be stony underfoot. You'll also pass through groves and villages with traditional architecture.

faqs.question: What makes this particular route notable?
faqs.answer: This trail follows an ancient Roman road, sometimes known as the "Pilgrim's Way" or "Via Trajana", offering a historical journey. It's also an alternative route on the Camino Francés, providing a quieter and less crowded experience compared to the main path, ideal for solitude and reflection.

faqs.question: Are there any significant landmarks or points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several points of interest. You'll experience the vastness of the Iberian Meseta. In Calzada del Coto, you can see the 17th-century Church of San Esteban of Calzada del Coto and the Hermitage of Our Lady of Perales. The villages also showcase traditional mud-built architecture.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route intersects with several other paths. It covers significant portions of the Camino Francés - 04 Burgos a León (48%) and Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas) (34%). You'll also find smaller sections of the Camino de Santiago (20%), Camino de Madrid - Melgar de Arriba -> Bercianos (1%), and others like Calle Norte 1 and Travesía de la Carretera 2º.

faqs.question: Where can I park or access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from El Burgo. While specific parking details aren't provided, El Burgo Ranero, being a common stop on the Camino Francés, typically has options for parking in or near the village. Public transport options to El Burgo Ranero are generally available, connecting it to larger towns in the region.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on the Camino Francés and its alternative routes, including this one. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially when passing through villages, agricultural areas, or near livestock. Ensure you carry enough water for your dog, as sources can be scarce on the open plains.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring (April-May) and early autumn (September-October) are generally the best times. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also offer pleasant temperatures for long-distance hiking across the open plains.

faqs.question: Are permits or entrance fees required for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, permits or entrance fees are not required to hike this section of the Camino Francés or its alternative routes. The paths are publicly accessible.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as this route is part of the Camino Francés, you will find amenities in the villages you pass through, such as El Burgo Ranero and Calzada del Coto. These typically offer cafes, small shops, and albergues (pilgrim hostels) or guesthouses for accommodation.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's length and difficulty, bring plenty of water, especially during warmer months, as shade and water sources can be limited. Sturdy hiking shoes are essential due to potentially stony sections. Sun protection (hat, sunscreen) is crucial on the open plains. Snacks, a map, and a first-aid kit are also recommended.
